data.title: Ross Gerber criticizes prediction markets as illegal sports betting, particularly targeting platform Kalshi due to violating state gaming laws. Marketed as futures contracts, he argues they should be shut down. Sports betting dominates Kalshi volume.
data.description: Renowned investor Ross Gerber criticized prediction markets likening them to illegal sports betting, pointing out that platforms like Kalshi are operating as unlicensed sports betting operations that violate state gaming laws. Gerber, the CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management, argued that these markets are allowed to exist despite being based on bets that should be classified as futures contracts, thus bypassing state laws in regions like California where sports betting remains illegal. Gerber's remarks came amid a legal crackdown on such platforms, with sports betting dominating the volume on Kalshi.

data.title: "5th Understanding China event in Guangzhou showcases youth perspectives on China in video format; held at Yuexiu Int'l Conf. Center."
data.description: A ceremony titled "China in the Eyes of the Young" was held at the Yuexiu International Conference Center in Guangzhou, China on December 2, 2025. This event, part of the 2025 Understanding China Conference, featured an international short video screening and award ceremony. The ceremony aimed to showcase perspectives on China from young individuals.

data.title: Vivoryon Therapeutics N.V. reports Q3 2025 financial results, company advances with EUR 5.1 million private placement, successful kidney function data, and plans to advance varoglutamstat in stage 3b/4 DKD. Varoglutamstat Phase 2 program shows significant improvement, raising hope for potential partnerships.
data.description: Vivoryon Therapeutics N.V. reported its Q3 2025 financial results, highlighting successful data analysis from the varoglutamstat Phase 2 program for patients with lower baseline eGFR, showing consistent treatment effects. The company also presented compelling kidney function data from the VIVIAD Phase 2b study at the ASN kidney week, following a successful private placement raising EUR 5.1 million. Vivoryon's CEO, Frank Weber, highlighted the advancements in varoglutamstat and financial flexibility for strategic partnerships. Key figures include a mean eGFR baseline level of 65-56 mL/min/1.73m² for patients with diabetes and a treatment effect range of 5.3-7.7 mL/min/1.73m²/year, supporting plans for a Phase 2b study in DKD. The company is exploring additional funding and partnership opportunities for future studies.

data.title: Torrid Holdings shares fall 14.5% to $1.12 in after-hours. Q3 net sales down 10.8% to $235.2M, comparable sales decline 8.3%. CEO comments.
data.description: Torrid Holdings Inc. (NYSE: CURV) saw its shares drop by 14.5% to $1.12 in after-hours trading following the release of its third-quarter fiscal results. The stock closed at $1.31, down 0.76%, on Wednesday. The California-based retailer reported a net loss of $6.4 million (equivalent to 6 cents per share) for the quarter ending on Nov. 1, compared to a net loss of $1.2 million (1 cent per share) in the same period last year. Net sales decreased by 10.8% to $235.2 million from $263.8 million, with comparable sales down by 8.3%. CEO Lisa Harper addressed the company's performance in light of these figures. For more details, please refer to the full story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Geely EX2 debuts in Thailand at 42nd Motor Expo, showcasing new-energy products like EX5.
data.description: Geely Auto made a notable appearance at the 42nd Thailand International Motor Expo held in Bangkok on December 4, 2025. The company showcased its latest range of new-energy vehicles, featuring the all-electric SUV GEELY EX5 and the plug-in hybrid SUV GEELY STARRAY EM-i. This unveiling generated significant interest among attendees at the expo, with a diverse lineup of sustainable transport solutions on display.
